revenue.evidence: RPG Revenue Vault monthlyRevenue is 0; 0 business revenue files were found under /root/ai-holding-company/businesses.
revenue.stage_rule: $0-$500 MRR: zero-cost businesses only; no paid SaaS/tools, no outbound execution

scoring_scale: 1-10 where 10 is best. For startup_cost, 10 means lowest cost. For fulfillment_complexity and lead_acquisition_difficulty, 10 means easiest.

sources.name: GCS Technologies - AI Security Policy Guide for Small Businesses (2026)
sources.note: SMBs often adopted ChatGPT/Copilot/Claude informally before policy, IT approval, vendor review, or visibility; policies should cover approved tools, prohibited data, review, approvals, and incidents.

sources.name: Microsoft Security Blog - Active AI agents, observability, governance, security (2026)
sources.note: Microsoft reports 80%+ of Fortune 500 use active AI agents and says agents need observability, governance, least privilege, owner accountability, and service-account-like controls.

sources.name: Miles IT - AEO & GEO Strategy Guide (2026)
sources.note: AI search rewards direct answers, structured trustworthy content, citation-worthy proof, and SEO foundations; do not promise rankings or citations.

sources.name: Kittl - 25 Best Digital Products to Sell in 2026
sources.note: Digital products have no inventory/shipping, can be created once and sold repeatedly, but require niche focus and quality packaging because the market is crowded.

sources.name: InsightAgent - Best Selling Digital Products on Etsy (2026)
sources.note: Printable/digital products can reach 85-95% margins; strongest categories include planners, business templates, educational worksheets, and bundles. Marketplace setup/payment remains a later approval step.

sources.name: Installed Agency Agents roster
sources.note: Local worker personas checked before role assignment; no custom roles required this run.

opportunities.name: Shadow AI Policy & Tool Inventory Sprint
opportunities.fit: Best $0-MRR beachhead. SMBs are already using AI informally and need practical visibility, allowed/prohibited-use rules, sensitive-data boundaries, and human review before risk becomes an incident.
opportunities.icp: 5-50 employee SMBs using ChatGPT, Copilot, Claude, browser agents, embedded AI, or no-code automations without a written AI-use policy or tool inventory.
opportunities.offer: Free 12-question Shadow AI Risk Self-Check; $499-$1,200 AI Policy & Tool Inventory Sprint; $500-$1,500/mo quarterly refresh, staff training, and evidence-log retainer after first sale.

workers.responsibility: Define sensitive-data categories, retention boundaries, and privacy-safe AI-use questions.
Keep wording conservative and route legal decisions to qualified humans.
Translate Shadow AI risks into practical SMB control checklists.
Create recurring evidence logs and review checklists.
Package self-checks, matrices, and owner briefing templates.

opportunities.next: Build self-check, tool inventory CSV, approved/prohibited matrix, sensitive-data rules, human-review checklist, and one-page policy skeleton.

opportunities.name: AI Agent Observability & Inventory Lite Sprint
opportunities.fit: Best adjacent/fresh offer. As agents/custom GPTs/no-code automations spread, owners need a simple list of what exists, who owns it, what it can access, which actions need approval, and what evidence should be kept.
opportunities.icp: SMBs/agencies experimenting with Copilot agents, custom GPTs, browser agents, n8n/Zapier automations, AI support bots, or no-code agents without owner maps or audit evidence.
opportunities.offer: Free AI Agent Visibility Checklist; $599-$1,500 Observability & Inventory Lite Sprint; $750-$1,750/mo quarterly agent inventory/evidence-log refresh after first sale.

workers.responsibility: Define governance fields, approval gates, and safe operating rules for agent workflows.
Map agent identity, owner, delegated authority, and proof-of-action requirements.
Apply least-privilege and visibility checks without performing scans.
Map sensitive data boundaries and retention constraints.
Design a simple inventory/evidence-log dashboard.
Package the inventory, map, and executive brief.

opportunities.next: Draft agent inventory CSV, owner/permission map, data-touch checklist, approval/rollback matrix, evidence-log template, and executive briefing locally.

opportunities.name: Customer Support Knowledge-Base & AI Triage Readiness Sprint
opportunities.fit: Strong zero-cost service for businesses that want AI support later but first need FAQ structure, routing, escalation, transparency, training, and measurement.
opportunities.icp: Small SaaS, ecommerce, appointment businesses, clinics, agencies, and local-service teams with repeated questions, inconsistent replies, weak FAQ pages, and no escalation rules.
opportunities.offer: Free Support AI-Readiness Checklist; $399-$899 KB/Triage Sprint; $500-$1,200/mo KB refresh/reporting retainer.

workers.responsibility: Define support outcomes, SLA rules, and customer-success reporting.
Draft canned replies and escalation language.
Create FAQ and help-center copy.
Build triage workflow and reporting cadence.
Collect public support-page gaps.

opportunities.next: Create support checklist, top-25 FAQ template, triage tree, escalation policy, canned responses, and weekly report template.

opportunities.name: AI Search Visibility & LLM Source-Map Retrofit Snapshot
opportunities.fit: Still viable but now runner-up. AEO/GEO sources show demand for structured direct answers and citation-worthy content, but the offer must avoid ranking/citation guarantees.
opportunities.icp: Local service businesses and small B2B firms with thin service pages, weak FAQs, unclear proof, no source map, and no AI-readable guidance.
opportunities.offer: Free 5-point AI Search Snapshot; $299-$799 retrofit plan; $300-$900/mo refresh/reporting retainer.

workers.responsibility: Score answerability and source clarity.
Score llms.txt/source-map/crawler guidance readiness.
Review crawlability and page hygiene.
Draft answer capsules and FAQs.
Collect public evidence snippets.

opportunities.next: Keep producing public-site samples and source-map examples, but prioritize Shadow AI for fastest close.

opportunities.name: Employee AI Training & Prompt SOP Workshop
opportunities.fit: Good upsell after governance pain is established. Staff need role-safe prompts, prompt logs, review workflows, and manager scorecards.
opportunities.icp: SMB owners who know staff use AI but need safe repeatable workflows for writing, support, research, proposals, HR/admin, marketing, and operations.
opportunities.offer: Free AI Skills Gap Self-Assessment; $399-$999 Prompt SOP Workshop; $300-$900/mo prompt-library refresh and office-hours retainer.

workers.responsibility: Build workshop agenda and exercises.
Draft role-safe prompts and testing notes.
Turn prompt use into approval gates and governance SOPs.
Embed sensitive-information guardrails.
Package deck, handouts, and logs.

opportunities.next: Draft one-hour workshop outline, 20 role-safe prompts, prompt review checklist, prompt log, and manager scorecard.

opportunities.name: AI Governance Template Micro-Store Assets
opportunities.fit: Useful productized derivative of the service templates. Digital-products research shows high-margin template bundles work, but marketplace/payment setup requires approval; until then, create local assets and listing copy only.
opportunities.icp: Solo consultants, fractional ops leaders, SMB owners, and agencies needing editable AI policy, tool inventory, prompt log, approval matrix, and training templates.
opportunities.offer: Local bundle draft now; future $19-$79 template pack and $199-$499 customization add-on after Brian approves sales channel/payment setup.

workers.responsibility: Package polished templates and export-ready docs.
Write listing copy and buyer instructions.
Add disclaimers and avoid legal-advice claims.
Build prompt/SOP templates.
Model bundle pricing and customization upsells.

opportunities.next: Package template files and listing draft only; do not open Etsy/Gumroad/Shopify/payment accounts.

selected_for_current_stage: Shadow AI Policy & Tool Inventory Sprint
AI Agent Observability & Inventory Lite Sprint
safety_boundary: No emails, calls, form submissions, scans, client-system access, paid tools, marketplace/payment signups, cron scheduling, or spending were performed.
